item.title: Re: Oh wow, memories!
item.content:encoded: /thumbsup

item.title: Oh wow, memories!
item.content:encoded:
To think I wrote this so long ago, to see it still here, immortalized, brings me so much joy.

Much love to everyone that supported my little armslug's article, and much love to this wonderful community for continuing to exist, and curate such wonderful fiction!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you're welcome!

armslug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the explanation! armslug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have one question, what is all this about armslugs?

The article states, "SCP-3243 appears to be a genetically-unique member of the species Limax maximus (great grey slug), measuring 3 meters long and 1 meter wide. SCP-3243 possesses two 1-meter-long forearm-like appendages, terminating in hands with two fingers and opposable thumbs […]"

the good boi is a slug with arms

armslug

(thank you very much for the nice comment and the +1!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love skips that are as curious of us as we are of them, makes them seem more alive in my opinion. I have one question, what is all this about armslugs?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This… this is a retired swamp god, isn't it?

Basically! Still a god, but more of the freelance consultant variety rather than fulltime.

No longer in charge, still has powers, and likes protecting beings.

Yep! Armslug is basically enjoying retirement but still notices and helps out kids who trip and fall on his lawn, so to speak.

So freaking friendly, without being forced…and so happy to teach!

Exactly! :3 Teaching means a lot to the Warden.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This… this is a retired swamp god, isn't it?
No longer in charge, still has powers, and likes protecting beings.
So freaking friendly, without being forced…and so happy to teach!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa! That's crazy, totally missed it. I feex. Thanks for the heads up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm unsure how this wasnt missed but you forgot an xxx at

SCP-XXXX is believed to subsist off of the metabolic outputs

Hope this helps

item.title: Re: "Care is to be taken to avoid inadvertently stepping on SCP-3243-1 instances."
item.content:encoded: Signs "lol get bodied"

item.title: Re: "Care is to be taken to avoid inadvertently stepping on SCP-3243-1 instances."
item.content:encoded: I imagine he just coated the researcher in a layer of mud as a "time out".

item.title: Re: "Care is to be taken to avoid inadvertently stepping on SCP-3243-1 instances."
item.content:encoded: The Green Warden's wrath was swift and just.

item.title: Re: "Care is to be taken to avoid inadvertently stepping on SCP-3243-1 instances."
item.content:encoded: I mean, mushrooms can grow back pretty fast. That one hat boi was probably fine, if smushed for a little.

item.title: "Care is to be taken to avoid inadvertently stepping on SCP-3243-1 instances."
item.content:encoded: Implying this happened. Foundation is evil and killers of armslug's hat bois >:(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The world may never know.

arrrrrmmmslugggmystery

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it possible to make a combination pith helmet and top hat? I know, a derby! A bulletproof derby. With some fancy decorations of leaves and fungi.

If I'm getttttting this right, Armslug is A: a manifested guardian of nature or B: a tribal shaman who was a badass in life and Armslug in the next. Righ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could see Lord Blackwood being gifted a righteous hat of pomp and circumstance if he asked that nicely

~~armslug~~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I say, m'good fellow, these gnomes of yours make jolly good servants! And this fabric you make is simply delightful! Would you mind making a debonair pith helmet of the stuff?"
Various hand gestures basically signifying "no problemo"

Also, aaaaarmsluuuug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They'd probably have a grand old time touring the Everglades and watching the gators

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to read a story of Lord Blackwood meeting this guy in the past.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: ~arrrrrrmsluuuuuugggg~

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded: Cool! Sentence added!

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded: Whoop, great as ever, Zyn!
Armslug thanks you all! ᕕ( ᐛ )ᕗ

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded:
I think we can add the following sentence in somewhere:

In addition to consuming swamp detritus, SCP-XXXX is believed to subsist off of the metabolic outputs of the photosynthetic plants on its back.

What do you think?

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded: It works out for me! I like the idea, although I'm not sure how we would work on that description.

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded:
Oooh, that's pretty cool! We might be able to add a sentence or two in with that explanation.

Bardeen, what do you think?

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded:
Re: Diet

Perhaps it uses a Coral like system(algae live inside coral polyps and provide food). Where it subsists off of the metabolic outputs of the photosynthetic plants on its back in addition to the plentiful detritus to be found in swamps.

This would also provide a biological/evolutionary justification for tending a slug-garden aside from adorableness

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded:
I would like to think that good ol' armslug photosynthesizes, mostly because of his eco-friendly attitude.

but maybe it's better to leave it to reader-interpretation.

^ this. Gives more of a sense of the other-worldliness of the armslug dude, but he definitely would eat enviro-friendly.

Also Lagtim3, thank you for the nice comment! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Armslug hands you an eco-friendly handkerchief.*

I kid, I kid.

item.title: Re: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded:
Omg I love everything you caught, armslug appreciates you

To respond to your questions, I would like to think that good ol' armslug photosynthesizes, mostly because of his eco-friendly attitude. But alas, I nor Zyn thought about that! Good catch, but maybe it's better to leave it to reader-interpretation.

Also! For your next proposition, I would love for you to doodle him! Although, in my eyes, The Green Warden is a not surprisingly white-gray slug, with spots all over his underside. As far as those go, I would say they are round and not very dense, just a few medium-sized ones. As far as your examples go for the spot type, I would say number 15. Just not as dense in the number of spots.

Edit:For the sake of fun, sample names for Fungimen: Hopper, Little Boy, Twin, Cleaner, Twiddle, Favorite.

item.title: Boy am I ever-glad I read this!
item.content:encoded:
My god, this has everything I love and more!

1.) Friendly, curious arthropod! Brownie points for using a traditionally "icky" arthropod that doesn't get nearly enough love <3
2.) He has a magical back-garden!
3.) Dr. Slugbert Armsworth, G.P.; I choose to believe he also mends injured everglades fauna.
4.) Fung-munculi helpers! And he names them!!
5.) ✧･ﾟ: *✧Armslug✧*:･ﾟ✧

Sorry, I try to put more thought into my feedback but this is entirely happy feel-good goodness and I love it. My only critiques (more like minor nitpicks) are that:

1.) I'd like to know more about his dietary habits. Per Wikipedia, "Limax maximus is omnivorous… a detrivore, cleaning up dead plants and fungi, and a carnivore known to pursue other slugs at a top speed of 6 inches per minute. It also eats young crops faster than they can grow…" So, what does a great grey slug this size eat? …Does he eat? Can he photosynthesize? Can he absorb nutrients from the detritus on his back the the shroomies don't eat?
2.) Per Wikipedia, these guys have the ability to create mucous strands durable enough to hang from (typically to mate). It'd be neat if he utilized his mucous, somehow, in what he does.

I'd really like to doodle this guy. Great Greys, funnily enough, come in a variety of colors and spot patterns; what would you say this guy looks like?

https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/54/Limax_maximus_variability.jpg/800px-Limax_maximus_variability.jpg <—— color/pattern examples

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Armslug hands you an eco-friendly handkerchief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm crying a single happy tear. This is wonderful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heartwarming (which I happen to need at the moment, so good timing!).

(ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧ εїз εїз εїз

And they are prepared to learn from it! I like all of that.

Glad you liked that! Thanks for the nice comment. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very good example of a benign SCP. Heartwarming (which I happen to need at the moment, so good timing!). Weird enough that the Foundation needs to keep the general public away, but no need for the Foundation to imprison it somewhere. And they are prepared to learn from it! I like all of that.

item.title: AHHH
item.content:encoded:
The amount of + on this SCP! I'm loving seeing the number skyrocket and i'm so very thankful for the community's support of this SCP!

So, in other news, I intend to write a Tale about our very own Dr. Hunter Miles, and his first meeting with good ol 3243!

News coming soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incidentally, someone in chat told us to change it from "and" to "or".

I've just gone and removed the botany part, since I guess it could be assumed that if the researchers are going to be conducting plant-based research as part of the cover story, they'd already be familiar with botany.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Distracting nitpick:

+ show block
– hide block

is currently staffed by three research personnel and one containment specialist with experience in botany or invertebrate biology.

That 'or' should be 'and', or it should be written as an order rather than description: "is to be staffed by three research personnel and one containment specialist with experience in botany or invertebrate biology."

armslug, armslug
in a swampy river
is a nice gift-giver
coz it is a chill slug

armslug, armslug
in a swampy river
pretty wise and pretty stout
has some mushroom servants helping out

item.title: Re: This is good
item.content:encoded:
Bardeen did a good with giving armslug his slug arms, hahaha. Glad you liked this!

#armslug

item.title: This is good
item.content:encoded:
You got a slug with arms to have smurf servants on its back… AND IT'S ADORABLE!!!

Instant +1

(Wow I never thought I'd think of a slug as adorable)

#armslug (let's get it trending)

Great scp!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: armslug and mushroom minions thank you for the +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hits the sweet spot for cute. Take the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! Reread! Share with your friends! This was the first SCP i directly took part in and I have to say it was an amazing experience! So, please get your friends to make wholesome scps, because i'm only one man and trying to convince peeps is a bit difficult lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the credit, but Zyn really made these little guys pop, they were a bit creepy in the original idea, but I was grateful when Zyn decided to flip that to adorablebois

Arrrrmm sluuug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Armslug thanks you!

Glad you liked the work! :D We put in a lot of little details (like the date that Dr. Miles received the enchanted life vest) so we hope that you enjoy the rereads as well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image of Armslug being groomed by a squad of squishy little Groot-like homonculi is delightful.

The little mushroom dudes were Bardeen's idea! I just made up the hats. d:

Thanks for the nice comment!

Armslug!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely great work. This is a SCiP I will reread again and again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: :) We spent a lot of time hashing out the little details to put in. Probably one of the longest ideas jam sessions I've had this year.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We were kind of going for "old, old god but more grandpa than conqueror figure". Glad you like it, and thanks for reading the draft in chat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a real fondness for wholesome SCPs that don't kill anyone. The image of Armslug being groomed by a squad of squishy little Groot-like homonculi is delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The more you get to know this guy and what he's about, the harder it is not to love him. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Armslug!

\o/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Armslug.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I appreciate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, an SCP that is intelligent and has a knack for wanting to protect humans. Interesting. One of my favorites to be recognized from now on. +1

Armslug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
nanananana

arm slug!

Thank you for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you think it's cute! It's certainly changed quite a bit from the original concept that Bardeen brought to the I&B forum.

Armslug!
